repo_notes.content: Infino is an embedded retrieval engine: it runs SQL, full-text (BM25), and vector search over a single copy of your data stored as standard Apache Parquet on object storage (Amazon S3, Azure Blob, or local disk). It runs in-process as a library — there is no server, daemon, or managed service. It is available as a Rust crate (cargo add infino), a Python wheel (pip install infino), and a Node.js package (npm install @infino-ai/infino). Document it as a product developers and AI agents use for retrieval: lead with how to install it and run a query (the public API), the multi-modal search surface, and the core concepts; treat the internal layers as supporting detail.

Common misconceptions to avoid when documenting Infino — prefer these corrected statements: (1) It does NOT need a server — it is embedded and runs in your process; you add it as a library and open a connection. (2) Its files are real Apache Parquet, not a proprietary format — DataFusion, DuckDB, and pyarrow can read a superfile's columns directly. (3) Hybrid search is ONE engine, not two — BM25, vector, and SQL run over the same copy of the data and compose into a single query; there is no separate keyword system and vector system to stitch together. (4) Storage is object storage, not a bundled database tier — data lives on S3, Azure Blob, or local disk.

Architecture: a 'superfile' is a single, valid Apache Parquet file with BM25 and vector index regions embedded alongside the columnar data. The 'supertable' layer composes many superfiles into one table with snapshot-isolated reads, append-only writes, and an atomic-commit manifest; the manifest carries term filters, value ranges, and vector centroids used to prune superfiles before any bytes are fetched. The public API is the small surface re-exported at the crate root (connect / connect_with, Connection, Supertable, IndexSpec, Metric, BoolMode, VectorSearchOptions, VectorFilter, InfinoError); the storage / superfile / supertable layers are internal (pub(crate)). Canonical design docs live in docs/architecture/ (overview, superfile, supertable); concept pages in docs/concepts/; operational answers in docs/faq.md; the honest limits in docs/tradeoffs.md. Infino's own performance numbers live in benches/README.md.
